CENTRAL BANK OF KENYA
Ref. No. CBK/DIR/DFR/2024/01
Date: 15 March 2024

TO: ALL INSTITUTIONS LICENSED UNDER THE BANKING ACT

CIRCULAR ON DIGITAL CREDIT PROVIDER COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS

Pursuant to the provisions of the Central Bank of Kenya Act (Cap 491) and the Banking Act
(Cap 488), the Central Bank of Kenya hereby issues the following directive to all institutions.

1. Purpose
The purpose of this circular is to provide guidelines on compliance requirements for digital
credit providers operating in Kenya. This circular supplements the Digital Credit Providers
Regulations, 2022 and is effective immediately upon issuance.

2. Scope
This circular applies to all institutions licensed as Digital Credit Providers (DCPs) under
the Central Bank of Kenya Act. It also applies to institutions that have applied for DCP
licenses and are operating under transition provisions.

3. Customer Data Protection Requirements
3.1 All DCPs must implement end-to-end encryption for customer data at rest and in transit.
3.2 Customer data must be stored within Kenya unless express written approval has been
obtained from the Central Bank of Kenya.
3.3 DCPs must appoint a dedicated Data Protection Officer within 60 days of receiving their license.
3.4 Breach notification to the Central Bank of Kenya must occur within 24 hours of detection.

4. Lending Conduct Requirements
4.1 Total cost of credit must be disclosed to borrowers before loan disbursement.
4.2 DCPs must not charge fees or penalties not disclosed in the loan agreement.
4.3 Rollover of loans shall only be permitted once per loan cycle with explicit written consent.
4.4 Debt collection practices must comply with the Consumer Protection Act.

5. Reporting Requirements
5.1 DCPs must submit monthly returns to the Central Bank of Kenya by the 15th of each month.
5.2 Quarterly audited financial statements must be submitted within 45 days of each quarter end.
5.3 Any material adverse development must be reported within 48 hours.

6. Compliance Timeline
All institutions must achieve full compliance within 90 days of the date of issuance.
Institutions requiring an extension must apply in writing to the Director, Digital Financial
Services, no later than 60 days from the date of this circular.

7. Penalties for Non-Compliance
Non-compliance with this circular may attract penalties as prescribed under Section 57 of
the Banking Act, including suspension or revocation of the DCP license.

Issued by authority of the Governor
Central Bank of Kenya
Nairobi, Kenya
